proven.claim: PROVEN: Return array has one VerificationResult per non-vacuous block
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P2, P5
; LINE: 116
; PROVEN: Return array has one VerificationResult per non-vacuous block
(declare-const total_blocks Int)
(declare-const vacuous_blocks Int)
(declare-const returned_results Int)
(assert (= total_blocks 5))
(assert (= vacuous_blocks 2))
(assert (= returned_results (- total_blocks vacuous_blocks)))
(assert (= returned_results 3))
(assert (not (= returned_results 3)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → returned results count = non-vacuous blocks exactly

proven.claim: REACHABLE: Second call to verifyAll sees different z3 availability
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P2, P4
; LINE: 116
; REACHABLE: Second call to verifyAll sees different z3 availability
; First call: z3 installed, returns sat/unsat
; Second call: z3 updated/broken, returns unknown/error
(declare-const first_call_z3_works Bool)
(declare-const second_call_z3_works Bool)
(declare-const first_result String)
(declare-const second_result String)
(assert (= first_call_z3_works true))
(assert (= first_result "sat"))
(assert (= second_call_z3_works false))
(assert (= second_result "unknown"))
; Temporal: same input smt2, different results
(assert (= first_result second_result))
(check-sat)
; sat → non-deterministic results across invocations if z3 state changes

proven.claim: REACHABLE: Race condition - isVacuous called multiple times, different results
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1, P2, P4
; LINE: 116
; REACHABLE: Race condition - isVacuous called multiple times, different results
; (in Node.js single-threaded this is theoretical, but same issue if cached results)
(declare-const smt2_block String)
(declare-const first_is_vacuous Bool)
(declare-const second_is_vacuous Bool)
(assert (= first_is_vacuous true))
(assert (= second_is_vacuous false))
; Same input, different results (e.g., regex state change, module reload)
(assert (= first_is_vacuous second_is_vacuous))
(check-sat)
; sat → non-deterministic isVacuous can cause inconsistent filtering
